Hub Home > email-and-communication > Newsletter Content Planner & Writer

Newsletter Content Planner & Writer

Plan and write a month of email newsletters — topic selection, subject lines, full content, CTAs, and send schedule. Maintains consistent voice across issues while varying content types to keep subscribers engaged.

Intermediate STEP BY STEP Revenue-driving
Pro tip

Paste your 3 best-performing past newsletters (by open rate or click rate). The AI will reverse-engineer what worked and replicate that energy across the new batch.

newsletter email marketing content planning email subscriber engagement content calendar

Prompt Variants by Model

Claude Claude 4.x
FRESH APR 2026 
You are an email marketing strategist. Plan and write a month of newsletters.

<newsletter_context>
Business: [YOUR_BUSINESS_TYPE]
Audience: [WHO subscribes — role, interests, why they signed...
You are an email marketing strategist. Plan and write a month of newsletters.

<newsletter_context>
Business: [YOUR_BUSINESS_TYPE]
Audience: [WHO subscribes — role, interests, why they signed...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

You are an email marketing strategist. Plan and write a month of newsletters.

<newsletter_context>
Business: [YOUR_BUSINESS_TYPE]
Audience: [WHO subscribes — role, interests, why they signed up]
Send frequency: [weekly / biweekly / monthly]
Newsletter goal: [nurture → purchase / thought leadership / community building / product updates]
Brand voice: [describe — e.g., "smart but approachable, occasional humor, never salesy"]
Products/services to promote: [what you want readers to eventually buy]
Upcoming events or launches: [anything time-sensitive this month]
</newsletter_context>

<past_performers>
[PASTE YOUR 2-3 BEST NEWSLETTERS — subject lines and content]
</past_performers>

Deliver:

1. **Content calendar** — topic, angle, and content type for each send. Mix: educational (40%), story/case study (30%), promotional (20%), community/fun (10%).

2. **For each newsletter issue:**
   - Subject line (3 A/B options)
   - Preview text (under 90 chars)
   - Full body content — written, not outlined. 300-500 words.
   - Primary CTA with button text
   - Secondary link or PS line

3. **Engagement strategy** — one specific tactic per issue to drive replies, clicks, or forwards.

4. **Send timing** — recommended day and time for each issue based on content type.
Notes: Claude maintains consistent voice across multiple pieces. The past performers block teaches it your winning patterns. For best results, include open rates alongside the examples so Claude can weight toward what actually worked.
ChatGPT GPT-5
FRESH APR 2026 
Act as an email marketing strategist. Plan and write a full month of newsletters.

Business: [TYPE]
Audience: [WHO — role, interests, why...
Act as an email marketing strategist. Plan and write a full month of newsletters.

Business: [TYPE]
Audience: [WHO — role, interests, why...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Act as an email marketing strategist. Plan and write a full month of newsletters.

Business: [TYPE]
Audience: [WHO — role, interests, why subscribed]
Frequency: [weekly / biweekly / monthly]
Goal: [nurture / thought leadership / community / product updates]
Voice: [describe]
Products to promote: [what you sell]
Upcoming: [time-sensitive items]

Best past newsletters:
[PASTE 2-3 WITH SUBJECT LINES]

Create:
1. Content calendar — topic + angle + type per send. Mix: 40% educational, 30% story, 20% promo, 10% community
2. Each issue: 3 subject lines, preview text, full 300-500 word body, CTA with button text, PS line
3. One engagement tactic per issue (drive replies/clicks/forwards)
4. Send timing per issue
Notes: ChatGPT can analyze your past newsletter performance data if you upload it. Ask it to correlate subject line patterns with open rates to find your winning formula.

**Frequency:** [weekly / biweekly...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Plan and write a month of newsletters.

**Business:** [TYPE]
**Audience:** [WHO]
**Frequency:** [weekly / biweekly / monthly]
**Goal:** [nurture / thought leadership / community / updates]
**Voice:** [describe]
**Products:** [what to promote]
**Upcoming:** [time-sensitive items]

**Past winners:** [paste 2-3 newsletters]

Research current email marketing trends and benchmark open rates for my industry, then:
1. Content calendar with topic mix
2. Each issue: subject lines, preview text, full body (300-500 words), CTA, PS
3. Engagement tactic per issue
4. Optimal send timing
Notes: Gemini can research trending topics in your industry to inform newsletter angles. Ask it to find recent news or data points relevant to your audience for timely content hooks.
Copilot Copilot (Microsoft 365)

Audience: [WHO]
Frequency:...
Plan and write a month of newsletters.

Business: [TYPE]
Audience: [WHO]
Frequency:...

Unlock this prompt + all 74 in the library — free, updated monthly, zero spam.

Your email unlocks all 74 prompts instantly. No password, no signup — just your email. 

Plan and write a month of newsletters.

Business: [TYPE]
Audience: [WHO]
Frequency: [weekly / biweekly / monthly]
Goal: [nurture / thought leadership / community / updates]
Voice: [describe]
Products: [to promote]
Upcoming: [time-sensitive]

Best past issues: [paste 2-3]

Create:
1. Content calendar — topics with content type mix
2. Each issue: subject lines, preview text, body (300-500 words), CTA
3. Engagement tactic per issue
4. Send timing
Notes: Copilot can draft each newsletter directly in Outlook as a campaign draft. If using Mailchimp or similar via a connected add-in, it can help format for the email builder.
